summary.direction: Warm artisan gallery on a single page with category-first reveal and inline product storytelling.
majorChanges: Hero and collections now open the page, with products hidden until a category is selected.
Category selection reveals product cards inline with smooth scroll and reveal animation.
Product selection expands an inline gallery/story showcase with CTA buttons.
WhatsApp is the dominant inquiry CTA across the page.
The palette shifted from dark indigo gradients to a warm light artisan theme.
About, Process, Contact, and Footer are part of the same long-form single-page flow.
remainingIssues: No deep-linking for selected category or product states.
No explicit lazy loading or responsive image optimization for remote catalog images.
Instagram contact defaults to a placeholder when site content is not populated.
The storefront logic remains concentrated in one large App component.
Some product descriptions still contain ecommerce-style wording.

buttons.primary: bg-[#A67C52] text-white rounded-full with hover to #8F6843
buttons.floatingWhatsApp: fixed bottom-right rounded-full bg-[#25D366] text-white with elevated shadow

homepage: Sticky header and floating WhatsApp button render immediately.
Hero text and two inquiry CTAs appear above the fold.
Collections render as category cards only.
Products are not visible on first load.
About, Process, Contact, and Footer are part of the same scrollable page.
categorySelection: The product region fades in with slight upward motion.
The page smooth-scrolls to the product section.
productSelection: User clicks a product card in the selected category.
An inline showcase appears below the grid.
Primary image and optional thumbnails render in the gallery area.
Story, materials, dimensions, customization, badges, and CTAs render in the detail column.
The page smooth-scrolls to the showcase area.
contact: WhatsApp opens a prefilled wa.me URL in a new tab.
Instagram opens the configured social link or # fallback.
The floating WhatsApp button uses the same prefilled wa.me destination.

components.name: App
components.purpose: Storefront shell, homepage flow, category reveal, inline product showcase, contact actions, and Supabase content loading

components.name: Admin
components.purpose: Admin login and management interface for site content, categories, products, and images

components.name: CategoryManager
components.purpose: Admin UI for adding, editing, deleting, and reassigning categories

components.name: ProductForm
components.purpose: Admin UI for creating a new product and selecting upload images

components.name: ProductList
components.purpose: Admin UI for editing existing products and embedding image controls

components.name: ImageManager
components.purpose: Admin UI for uploading, selecting, reordering, and deleting existing product images

performance.imageOptimizations: Remote Supabase public image URLs are used directly; no responsive image variants or explicit lazy-loading attributes are present.
performance.bundleImpact: Build output includes a JS bundle of about 206 KB uncompressed / 59 KB gzip and a CSS bundle of about 23.9 KB uncompressed / 4.8 KB gzip.

accessibility.altTextSupport: Present for category and product images.
accessibility.focusStates: Native browser focus states are available; no custom focus treatment is applied in the storefront.
accessibility.contrastConsiderations: Primary text on the light background has strong contrast; muted gray supporting text is used throughout for secondary information.

responsiveness.desktop: Category cards render in a multi-column grid; product showcase becomes a two-column layout on large screens.
responsiveness.tablet: Cards reduce columns as width narrows; product showcase remains stacked until large breakpoints.
responsiveness.mobile: Single-column stacking is used for the homepage flow, category cards, product cards, and product showcase.
knownIssues: The page is long because the About, Process, Contact, and Footer sections remain on the same route.
The sticky header and floating WhatsApp CTA remain present on mobile throughout scroll.
No route-based state preservation exists for category or product selection.
Some product descriptions still contain ecommerce-style wording.
Instagram contact defaults to a placeholder when no link is configured.
No deep-linking exists for category or product states.
No lazy loading or responsive image pipeline is implemented.
Storefront logic remains concentrated in one large App component.
